news.title: Olympus pushes deeper into oncology with $270M BioProtect deal
news.snippet: Olympus is pushing deeper into prostate cancer care with the acquisition of BioProtect, an Israeli company whose biodegradable balloon spacer has been used in more than 11,000 procedures worldwide.

news.title: Kura posts Krazati combo data on drug designed to unleash KRAS inhibitors’ potential
news.snippet: Kura Oncology has published early data on darlifarnib in combination with Bristol Myers Squibb’s Krazati, linking the drug candidate to response rates of up to 69% when given with the KRAS inhibitor.

news.snippet: Faced with a future of evaporating Xtandi sales, Astellas has set out ambitions to license fresh assets while also continuing to tighten its belt.

news.title: Has Novartis’ T-Charge been overtaken by in vivo CAR-Ts? Execs argue there’s room for both

news.snippet: A phase 2b trial of Biogen and Denali Therapeutics’ Parkinson’s disease candidate has missed its primary endpoint, prompting the partners to end development in the idiopathic form of the condition.<br

news.title: Betting on biotech: Prediction markets set sights on clinical trials
news.snippet: Several biotech contracts are rotating through Kalshi and Polymarket, while a prediction market specifically focused on clinical trials has recently launched.

news.snippet: The potential $7 billion buyout deal in the radiopharmaceutical space could be weeks away, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

news.snippet: Two years after launching its “More To Parkinson’s” campaign with actor Ryan Reynolds, Acadia Pharmaceuticals is asking Reynolds to suit up once again as it broadens the project.

news.snippet: A three-way battle among TROP2 ADCs in first-line triple-negative breast cancer is forming as AstraZeneca and Daiichi Sankyo's Datroway became the first to cross the FDA's finish line.

news.title: Ebola risk raised to 'very high' in DR Congo
news.snippet: The head of the UN health agency says the risk in the wider region is "high", but it remains "low" at the global level.

news.title: UK scientists developing Ebola vaccine that could be ready for trials in months
news.snippet: The rare species of Ebola involved - known as Bundibugyo - kills around a third of those infected and has no proven vaccine yet.

news.title: Riskiest skin cancer cases hit UK record high
news.snippet: The number of melanoma skin cancer cases has risen above 20,000 a year for the first time in the UK.

news.title: MPs call for sunbed advertising ban to help prevent skin cancer
news.snippet: A report by a cross-party group of MPs has found the majority of skin cancer cases are preventable.
